course_description:
Analysis of cultural heritage in legislative, practical and ethical frameworks. Cultural aspects of environmental management, including site management, fire management, traditional use of parks and natural resources, and interpretation planning. Review of cultural landscapes concept within a broad conservation framework.

This course examines the principles of cultural heritage management in today's world.ﾠIssues of stakeholder engagement in heritage management and the role of legislation and policy are discussed.

You will learn the practical steps involved in the preparation of a heritage impact assessment. Case studies of cultural heritage management are provided. The links between nature and culture in heritage and protected area management are examined. The relationships between local, state, national and international heritage (especially World Heritage) are reviewed.

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:

The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
course_aims:
The principal aim of the course is to ensure that students understand the place of cultural heritage management in planning, environmental management and planning, and protected area management. To achieve this aim, the course has a number of related subsidiary aims that will assist students to:

1. develop an understanding of the concept of cultural heritage

2. understand new directions in cultural heritage management as they relate to planning and environmental management.

3. understand significance assessment and what is involved in the development of a cultural management plan.

4. understand what is involved in preparing a heritage management plans for cultural heritage places.

learning_outcomes.description: demonstrate an understanding of the concept of cultural heritage
discuss new directions in cultural heritage management
demonstrate an understanding of the basic steps required in planning for cultural heritage management
demonstrate an understanding of the basic steps in preparing heritage management plans and impact statements for cultural heritage places

assessment_details.title: Online Quiz
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will complete one online quiz within a set timeframe. The quiz will consist of 3 shorts answer questions worth 5% each. A time limit will be set and you must complete the quiz within that time. The quiz will cover week 1 to 7. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Assessments will be completed online through Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date. For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Heritage Impact Assessment (Group Report)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessment factor. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: You will form groups of 4 to 5 students to create a heritage impact assessment report for a proposed development in Brisbane. Further details of this assessment will be made available on Blackboard. In addition, you will be asked to assess the contribution made by your fellow group members to the assessment task using a peer assessment tool. Those students who do not make a satisfactory contribution to the work may be marked down. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The exam will cover lectures from weeks 1 - 11 and will consist of both MCQs and short answer questions. This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
